# tela

> The team wiki your AI agents can actually read and write — through a built-in MCP server, on markdown you self-host. Live multiplayer, instant SQLite full-text search, one binary. Your data, your server.

tela is an open-source, self-hostable, **agent-native** team wiki. It ships `tela-mcp`, a published Model Context Protocol server that gives agents 17 scoped tools over the same REST API the UI uses — create, read, update, and search pages, leave anchored comments, import markdown, manage spaces. Pages are canonical markdown forever (no block model, no proprietary format). Go + SQLite/FTS5 backend, React/Milkdown editor, Yjs live collaboration.

## Product
- [Home](https://tela.cagdas.io/): what tela is, the agent/MCP story, the feature showcase, an honest comparison vs Notion/Confluence/Obsidian/Outline, and self-host instructions.
- [Live instance](https://tela.cagdas.io/login): the running app.

## Code & packages
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/zcag/tela): open source — Go backend (SQLite + FTS5), React/Milkdown frontend, and the MCP server.
- [tela-mcp on npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tela-mcp): the published MCP server.
- [MCP server docs](https://github.com/zcag/tela/blob/main/mcp/README.md): the full tool catalog and the `.mcp.json` configuration.

## For agents
Point any MCP client (Claude, Cursor, or your own agent) at a tela instance with a scoped personal access token (`tela_pat_…`, read / write / admin, pinnable to one space). The agent then reads the team's docs, writes back what it learns, searches, and comments — as a first-class participant, not a chat bolt-on.

## Optional
- [llms-full.txt](https://tela.cagdas.io/llms-full.txt): the same information with the full positioning, feature list, and FAQ.
